title: States Weekly: June 24, 2026
gist: CSIS's Aryan D'Rozario rounds up India's state-level policy actions for the week of June 24, 2026: Kerala rolls out a $26,416-per-family health insurance scheme, Assam moves to list two state power utilities to raise capital, Chhattisgarh hikes electricity tariffs 6.23%, Gujarat and Maharashtra launch new advanced-manufacturing and automation industrial policies, and Bihar raises rural land valuation rates 60% alongside a stamp-duty increase.

insights.claim: Kerala's new Oommen Chandy Health Insurance Scheme provides state-backed health-cost support of up to $26,416 (INR 25 lakh) per family.
insights.speaker: Aryan D'Rozario
insights.so_what: A large state-funded health insurance expansion — relevant for Indian health-insurance and hospital-services demand forecasting in Kerala.
insights.quote: The scheme will provide support of up to $26,416 (INR 25 lakh) per family.
insights.locator: Kerala announces the Oommen Chandy Health Insurance Scheme.

insights.claim: Jharkhand launched a 'District Sandbox' initiative creating a real-world testing environment for innovators, startups, and researchers to trial medical technologies in public health settings before large-scale deployment.
insights.speaker: Aryan D'Rozario
insights.so_what: A regulatory sandbox lowers go-to-market friction for health-tech startups in India — a state to watch for early medtech pilots and public-private testing deals.
insights.quote: The initiative aims to create a real-world testing environment, enabling innovators, startups, and researchers to test medical technologies in public health settings before large-scale deployment.

insights.claim: Assam announced plans to list two state-owned power utilities — Assam Electricity Grid Corporation Limited (AEGCL) and Assam Power Generation Corporation Limited (AGCL) — on the stock exchange to access capital markets and raise resources.
insights.speaker: Aryan D'Rozario
insights.so_what: A potential new state-utility IPO/listing pipeline in India — worth tracking for Indian power-sector equity supply and state-level utility privatization momentum.
insights.quote: The decision aims to enable these corporations to access capital markets and raise resources.
insights.locator: Assam announces plans to list the Assam Electricity Grid Corporation Limited

insights.claim: Chhattisgarh's state electricity regulator (CSERC) approved an average 6.23% electricity tariff increase across all consumer categories, effective July 1, 2026.
insights.speaker: Aryan D'Rozario
insights.so_what: A direct read on Indian state power-utility revenue and cost-recovery trends — relevant to power-sector margins and inflation pass-through in Chhattisgarh.
insights.quote: approves an average electricity tariff increase of 6.23 percent across all consumer categories, effective July 1, 2026.
insights.locator: Chhattisgarh releases the CSERC Tariff Order for Financial Year 2026-27.

insights.claim: Delhi's draft DERC regulations aim to widen eligibility for Green Energy Open Access based on a consumer's sanctioned load or contracted demand, making renewable power procurement accessible to a larger pool of electricity consumers.
insights.speaker: Aryan D'Rozario
insights.so_what: Expands the addressable market for corporate and commercial renewable power procurement in Delhi — a tailwind for renewable-energy suppliers and open-access power traders.
insights.quote: aim to make Green Energy Open Access more accessible to a larger number of electricity consumers, based on the sanctioned load or contracted demand of the consumer.
insights.locator: Delhi issues Draft DERC (Terms and Conditions for Green Energy Open Access)

insights.claim: Gujarat's Viksit Gujarat Industrial Policy 2026 sets out a framework aiming to establish the state as a global leader in advanced manufacturing, innovation, and sustainable development.
insights.speaker: Aryan D'Rozario
insights.so_what: Signals Gujarat's continued push to attract advanced-manufacturing FDI and capex — relevant for site-selection and industrial-policy comparisons across Indian states.
insights.quote: sets out a framework which aims to establish Gujarat as a global leader in advanced manufacturing, innovation, and sustainable development.
insights.locator: Gujarat announces the Viksit Gujarat Industrial Policy 2026.

insights.claim: Maharashtra approved the Maharashtra Automated Systems Policy 2026 to accelerate growth across the entire automation ecosystem, covering R&D, manufacturing, deployment, and maintenance/repair.
insights.speaker: Aryan D'Rozario
insights.so_what: A dedicated state industrial policy for automation and robotics — an early signal of where India's robotics-manufacturing investment will cluster.
insights.quote: The policy seeks to accelerate growth across the entire automated systems ecosystem, covering research and development, innovation, manufacturing, deployment, operations, maintenance and repair.
insights.locator: Maharashtra approves the Maharashtra Automated Systems Policy 2026.

insights.claim: Bihar raised rural land circle rates (the Minimum Value Register) by 60% and the general stamp duty rate by 1 percentage point, while widening the total stamp-duty concession for land registered in a woman's name to 0.5%.
insights.speaker: Aryan D'Rozario
insights.so_what: A sharp repricing of official rural land values raises registration costs and the property-tax base — material for real-estate and land-acquisition cost models in Bihar.
insights.quote: rural land rates will increase by 60 percent.
insights.locator: Bihar announces MVR revisions alongside the Bihar Stamp

insights.claim: Maharashtra revised its Right to Information fee schedule, tripling both the application fee (INR 10 to 30) and the per-page cost of obtaining document copies (INR 2 to 5).
insights.speaker: Aryan D'Rozario
insights.so_what: A minor but telling data point on rising administrative-transparency costs in Maharashtra; low direct investor materiality but useful governance-trend context.
insights.quote: the cost of obtaining copies of documents has increased from $0.02 to $0.05 (INR 2 to 5) per page.
insights.locator: Maharashtra revises Maharashtra Right to Information Rules, 2026
